title: Peloton Q1 2024 Earnings: Wider Loss than Expected
text: Peloton Q1 FY2024 Earnings Report. Financial Performance: Peloton reported a loss of 44 cents per share, wider than the 34 cents expected by analysts. Revenue came in at $595.5 million versus $591 million expected, representing a narrow beat on the top line but miss on profitability. Subscriber Challenges: The connected fitness company continued to lose members and failed to make money off existing subscribers, indicating deteriorating unit economics. Connected fitness subscriptions declined year-over-year, signaling continued churn and subscriber loss. Analyst Perspective: Wall Street expected the company to lose 67 cents per share on revenue of $637.03 million for the full fiscal year, compared to the year-ago quarter when the loss was $1.10 per share on revenue of $805.2 million. Full Year Expectations: For the full year ending July 2023, the company was expected to lose $2.07 per share, better than a $7.79 loss a year ago, while full-year revenue was expected to decline 14.7% year over year to $3.06 billion. This reflected the ongoing transition from growth to profitability.
